channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Polish leader Tusk plans large-scale military training for all adult males to boost reserves
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk said Friday that his government is working on a plan to prepare large-scale military training for every adult male in response to the changing security situation in Europe.
item.dc:creator: VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia attacks Ukraine's energy supplies as US cuts its access to satellite images
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia attacked Ukrainian energy facilities with dozens of missiles and drones during the night, officials said Friday, hobbling the country’s ability to deliver heat and light to its citizens and to power weapons factories vital to its defenses.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV and JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Clashes in Syria between government forces and Assad loyalists kill nearly 150
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Fighters siding with Syria's new government stormed three villages near the country's coast, killing dozens of men in response to recent attacks on government security forces by loyalists of ousted President Bashar Assad, a war monitor said.
item.dc:creator: BY BASSEM MROUE and ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump weighs new sanctions on Russia, days after pausing military aid and intel sharing with Ukraine
item.description: President Donald Trump says he is “strongly considering” levying new sanctions and tariffs on Russia for its war against Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani And Chris Megerian, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 90,000 Palestinians attend the first Friday prayers of Ramadan in Jerusalem
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — In the first Friday prayers of the Muslim holy month of Ramadan, about 90,000 Palestinians prayed at the Al-Aqsa Mosque compound in Jerusalem’s Old City under tight security by Israeli forces.
item.dc:creator: IMAD ISSEID and JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Takeaways from the EU's landmark security summit after Trump said Europe must fend for itself
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — European Union leaders are trumpeting their endorsement of a plan to free up hundreds of billions of euros to inject into their defense budgets after the Trump administration warned that the continent must look after its own security, including Ukraine, in future.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK and RAF CASERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Far-right front-runner in Romania’s annulled presidential race files his candidacy in the rerun
item.description: Romanian far-right populist Calin Georgescu has filed his candidacy in a rerun of last year's presidential election that was annulled by a top court after his surprise first-round win.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Pope Francis still receiving oxygen therapy, will not read Sunday prayers: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis is still receiving oxygen therapy and will not be able to read Sunday prayers, according to the Vatican. The pontiff is now entering his fourth week in Rome's Gemelli Hospital.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Huge unexploded World War II-era bomb found in crowded Paris residential area
item.description: A half-ton World War II-era bomb found by workers near train tracks outside of Paris is disrupting train and road travel across the region.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Aid efforts in Gaza slow as millions of promised USAID dollars do not arrive, agency officials say
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — The Trump administration’s cuts to USAID have frozen hundreds of millions of dollars in contractual payments to aid groups, leaving them paying out of pocket to preserve a fragile ceasefire, according to officials from the U.S. humanitarian agency.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: UN prepping for spending cuts as DOGE roots out waste in US, internal docs show
item.description: Fox News Digital obtained an internal United Nations memo indicating that the international body is preparing heads of departments for funding cuts as DOGE roots out waste.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Norway's crown princess condition worsens as she battles lung disease
item.description: Norwegian Crown Princess Mette-Marit's chronic lung disease is affecting "her ability to perform her duties," the Royal House of Norway announced.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UN says 186 missing and at least 2 dead after migrant boats capsize off Yemen and Djibouti
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — At least two people have died and 186 others are missing after four boats carrying migrants from Africa capsized overnight in waters off Yemen and Djibouti, the U.N.'s migration agency said on Friday.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republicans press Trump to resume military, intelligence aid to Ukraine
item.description: Republican lawmakers are starting to urge President Trump to reverse his decisions to pause U.S. military and intelligence assistance to Ukraine, warning that a prolonged stoppage of U.S. help for the war effort would have a seriously detrimental effect.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, The Hill

item.title: Court orders South Korean President Yoon released from jail for his criminal trial over martial law
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — A South Korean court on Friday ordered impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol to be released from jail, a move that could allow Yoon to stand trial for his rebellion charge without being physically detained. Yoon was arrested and indicted in January over the Dec.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Boy with loaded shotgun boarded plane in Australia, tackled by passenger
item.description: A 17-year-old boy with a loaded shotgun boarded a plane at an Australian airport before being restrained by a pilot and two passengers, police said Friday.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: South Korea stops some military drills after accidental bombing in possible setback to US exercises
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea suspended the training flights of air force aircraft and all live-fire drills following its fighter jets’ accidental bombing of a civilian area, officials said Friday, posing a potential setback to its upcoming annual military training with the United States.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Albania issues yearlong TikTok ban amid youth violence concerns
item.description: The Albanian Cabinet decided in favor of a 12-month TikTok shutdown Thursday, blaming the app for inciting bullying and violence, especially among the country's youth.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US, Ukraine planning peace talks in Saudi Arabia: Witkoff
item.description: U.S. and Ukrainian officials are talking to set up a meeting next week in Saudi Arabia aimed at structuring the framework for a ceasefire with Russia and a peace agreement, President Trump’s special envoy Steve Witkoff told reporters on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Hamas brushes off Trump's threat and says it will only free hostages in return for a lasting truce
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Hamas on Thursday brushed off President Donald Trump's latest threat and reiterated that it will only free the remaining Israeli hostages in exchange for a lasting ceasefire in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: In tense relations with India, Pakistani TV dramas break down barriers that diplomacy often cannot
item.description: KARACHI, Pakistan (AP) — Two Pakistani women sit together on a couch, rehearsing their lines while a director scrutinizes them. Waiting off camera for his scene is the male lead, an actor blessed with “Bachelor” hair and fine bone structure.
item.dc:creator: RIAZAT BUTT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Top Hong Kong court overturns convictions of 3 former organizers of Tiananmen vigils
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — Hong Kong's top court on Thursday overturned the convictions of three former organizers of an annual vigil in remembrance of the 1989 Tiananmen Square crackdown over their refusal to provide information to police, marking a rare victory for the city's pro-democracy activists.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mother of Abbey Gate victim says Biden ‘robbed’ family of justice
item.description: "If we can take one terrorist off the list of harming Americans, especially our soldiers, then that is a good day," Paula Knauss Selph told NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Trump issues 'last warning' to Hamas to release all remaining hostages held in Gaza
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Wednesday issued what he called a “last warning” to Hamas to release all remaining hostages held in Gaza, directing a sharply worded message after the White House confirmed that he had recently dispatched an envoy for unprecedented direct talks with the mi
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Dems' response to ISIS leader arrest 'disturbing,’ says father of Marine killed at Abbey Gate
item.description: Father of fallen Marine Jared Schmitz, who was killed at Abbey Gate by an ISIS-K bomber, was "disturbed" when Democrats refused to applaud the arrest of the terrorist involved in the attack.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What Trump’s aid freeze means for Ukraine war
item.description: President Donald Trump’s freeze on U.S. aid going to Ukraine threatens to create severe problems for Kyiv in its war against Russia, leaving the country more vulnerable to aerial attacks on population centers and key infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, WGN 9

item.title: China will increase its defense budget 7.2% this year
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — China said Wednesday it will increase its defense budget 7.2% this year, as it continues its campaign to build a larger, more modern military to assert its territorial claims and challenge the U.S. defense lead in Asia.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER BODEEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Arab leaders endorse Egypt's plan to rebuild Gaza as an alternative to Trump's proposal
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Arab leaders on Tuesday endorsed Egypt's postwar plan for the Gaza Strip that would allow its roughly 2 million Palestinians to remain, in a counterproposal to U.S. President Donald Trump’s plan to depopulate the territory and redevelop it as a beach destination.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and MOHAMMAD JAHJOUH, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 12 killed, 30 wounded in Pakistan military base attack launched by suicide bombers
item.description: PESHAWAR, Pakistan (AP) — Two suicide bombings breached a wall at a military base in northwestern Pakistan before other attackers stormed the compound and were repelled in violence that killed at least 12 people and wounded 30 others, according to officials and a local hospital.
item.dc:creator: RIAZ KHAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Pressured by Trump’s Gaza plan, Arab countries meet in Egypt to discuss alternative proposal
item.description: Arab nations met for a summit in Cairo, Egypt, on Tuesday with the goal of developing a counterproposal to Trump's Gaza plan, aiming to rebuild without displacing residents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis suffers new breathing crises, is back on noninvasive ventilation, Vatican says
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis suffered two new acute respiratory crises Monday and was put back on noninvasive mechanical ventilation, in another setback to his battle to fight pneumonia, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: Trump and his allies lash out at Ukraine’s Zelenskyy and suggest he may need to resign
item.description: President Donald Trump ’s senior aides and allies lashed out at Ukrainian leader Volodymyr Zelenskyy from Washington as he attended a European summit in London to rally international support for his military’s fight against the Russian invasion.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump again attacks Zelenskyy with potential peace deal in limbo
item.description: There is a growing chorus of Republicans calling for new Ukrainian leadership following Friday's Oval Office meeting.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Israel cuts off Gaza aid to pressure Hamas to accept a new ceasefire proposal
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israel stopped the entry of all goods and supplies into the Gaza Strip on Sunday and warned of “additional consequences” if Hamas doesn’t accept a new proposal to extend a fragile ceasefire, while key mediator Egypt accused Israel of using “starvation as a weapon.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israel supports a US proposal to extend Gaza ceasefire's first phase. But Hamas wants Phase 2
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Israel’s government said early Sunday it supports a proposal to extend the first phase of the ceasefire in Gaza through Ramadan and Passover, though Hamas has insisted on negotiating the truce’s second phase instead.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation
